summary: Created acceptance test suite for US-041 shader-based vertex displacement. All 32 tests pass, verifying: simplex noise (snoise/fbm3) is present in both pointCloud and ribbonField shaders, layeredNoise is removed, uDisplacementScale uniform exists and derives from motionAmplitude * structureComplexity, bass/treble energy uniforms drive GPU displacement, deformation evolves over time via uTime, no CPU per-point loops (position buffer version unchanged), quality tier scaling works (1/2/3 octaves), existing displacement layers (radial expansion, twist, treble jitter, pointer repulsion, ribbon sway) are preserved, reduced motion calms displacement, and no forbidden storage APIs are accessed. The shader implementation was already complete from prior work — noise3d.glsl provides snoise() and fbm3(), both vertex shaders use simplex FBM for sculptural displacement, and both TypeScript systems wire uDisplacementScale correctly.
